game_name: Arcanum: Of Steamworks and Magick Obscura

design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore open world, interact with NPCs, accept quests with multiple solution paths (combat, persuasion, thievery, bribery, stealth, diplomacy), gain experience and followers, level character skills and attributes
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Magic vs. Technology - players must choose specialization path that grants strengths but introduces mechanical vulnerabilities (tech users become magic-resistant but suffer equipment malfunctions; magic users gain spell power but tech items fail frequently)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Shape a unique character with radical build diversity (pure tech, pure magic, or hybrid neutral), solve problems through diverse playstyles, recruit a party with moral/alignment consequences for player conduct
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commitment to player agency through consequence-driven companion systems where followers leave or attack if the player's moral conduct offends their values; hard magic-technology dichotomy that creates real mechanical tradeoffs rather than cosmetic choice

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Combat can be avoided entirely through stealth or diplomacy; player specialization choice (magic vs. tech vs. hybrid) fundamentally alters encounter difficulty and resource availability

progression.upgrade_system: Level-based attribute and skill improvement via character points (1 point per level, +1 bonus every 5th level for total of 64 points across game); crafting system for technological path allowing custom weapon/ammunition/item construction
progression.unlock_mechanism: Quest completion grants experience and follower recruitment based on player aptitudes and alignment; specialization meter gates access to magic/tech effectiveness (high tech = magic immunity but magic weakness; high magic = spell power but tech malfunction)
replayability_hooks: Multiple quest solutions enabling different playstyles
Radically different character builds (pure tech, pure magic, hybrid neutral)
Companion recruitment varies by player conduct and alignment
Custom map editor with included Vormantown module plus official modules

world_building_techniques: Industrial Revolution setting with fantasy races undergoing technological transformation
Moral consequence system where NPC followers react to player conduct

faction_indicators: Magic vs. Technology alignment system expressed through character specialization and NPC faction relationships
Moral alignment system affecting companion loyalty and party composition
danger_signals: Unaligned companion departure when player conduct violates their moral code
Companion combat engagement against player when ethics sufficiently violated
environmental_storytelling: Continental world with three islands inhabited by Tolkien-influenced races (humans, elves, dwarves, gnomes, halflings, orcs, ogres)
Zeppelin crash narrative hook introducing conflict with ancient power
